paper_title: Archaeobotanical Methods for Palaeovegetation and Human Interaction Reconstruction

methods.description: Systematic strategies for collecting and processing archaeological sediment samples to recover plant remains, combining hand-picking of large objects with water-based processing techniques like flotation, sieving, wash-over, and water-screening, using various mesh sizes to separate organic and non-organic fractions.

steps.description: Apply systematic sediment sampling strategies at each site (e.g., probabilistic, per grid quadrant, from profiles).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform systematic archaeological sediment sampling

steps.description: Combine hand-picking of large objects (mainly wood and charcoal fragments) with sediment processing with water for the recovery of smaller organic remains.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate large objects by hand and process sediment with water

steps.description: Process sediment in a flotation tank (at Cova del Sardo and Coves del Fem).
steps.searchable_summary: Process sediment using flotation tank

steps.description: Sieve sediment with the wash-over technique (at La Draga).
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve sediment using wash-over technique

steps.description: Water-screen samples for the recovery of larger items (>2 mm) (at La Draga).
steps.searchable_summary: Water-screen sediment for large items

steps.description: Perform dry screening (at Coves del Fem).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform dry screening of sediment

steps.description: Use mesh sizes (4, 2, 1, 0.5 and 0.3 mm) to collect the heavy fraction (non-organic material) and the light fraction (of organic remains).
steps.searchable_summary: Separate heavy and light fractions using specific mesh sizes

methods.description: Identification of archaeological seeds based on morphometrical criteria and surface observation using a stereomicroscope, compared against reference collections and identification atlases.

steps.description: Observe morphometrical criteria of the seed.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe and record seed morphometrical criteria

steps.description: Observe the seed surface.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe and record seed surface features

steps.description: Use a stereomicroscope with a 6.5−50× magnification range for observation.
steps.searchable_summary: Use stereomicroscope for seed observation

steps.description: Compare observations with reference collection material.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare seed features with reference collection

steps.description: Compare observations with identification atlases for wild and cultivated plants.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare seed features with identification atlases

methods.description: Standard procedure for identifying wood and charcoal species by observing anatomical patterns in three sections using reflected or transmitted light microscopy and comparing with modern wood samples and anatomy atlases.

steps.description: Observe the anatomical patterns of the wood/charcoal sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe anatomical patterns of wood or charcoal

steps.description: Observe anatomical patterns along three anatomical sections (transversal, longitudinal tangential, and longitudinal radial).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and observe three anatomical sections (transversal, tangential, radial)

steps.description: Use a reflected light microscope equipped with bright/dark field (BF-DF) for charcoal and transmitted light for wood samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Use reflected or transmitted light microscope for observation

steps.description: Use magnification factors between 40× and 400×.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform microscopic observation at 40x-400x magnification

steps.description: Compare archaeological samples with modern wood.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare archaeological wood/charcoal anatomy with modern wood

steps.description: Compare archaeological samples with wood anatomy atlases.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare archaeological wood/charcoal anatomy with anatomy atlases

methods.description: Analysis of wooden artifacts to identify raw material, characterize technological processes based on surface marks, record age and diameters, and study dendro-anthracological factors like minimum diameter using qualitative and quantitative methods.

steps.description: Identify the raw material from its anatomical features.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify wood raw material from anatomical features

steps.description: Characterize the technological processes based on the marks observed on the artifact surface.
steps.searchable_summary: Characterize technological processes from artifact surface marks

steps.description: Record the age of piles and planks.
steps.searchable_summary: Record age of wooden piles and planks

steps.description: Record the diameters of piles and planks.
steps.searchable_summary: Record diameter of wooden piles and planks

steps.description: Study dendro-anthracological factors, such as the minimum diameter of the wood.
steps.searchable_summary: Study dendro-anthracological factors like minimum diameter

steps.description: Perform qualitative analysis based on the curvature of the growth rings.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform qualitative analysis of growth ring curvature

steps.description: Perform quantitative approaches using new image analysis tools for minimum diameter calculation.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform quantitative image analysis for minimum diameter calculation

steps.description: Calculate minimum diameters using the morphology of the growth rings (circle method) or trigonometrical methods of the convergence of the radiuses of the wood.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate minimum diameter using circle method or trigonometrical methods

methods.description: Identification of vegetal fibers using optical microscopy (OM) and scanning electron microscopy (SEM) to observe microanatomical features, requiring specific sample preparation for SEM, and comparison with modern materials and technical literature.

steps.description: Obtain minimum-sized samples, usually fragments broken during excavation and restoration processes.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain minimum-sized vegetal fiber samples

steps.description: Observe transversal sections using a reflected BF-DF optical microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe transversal sections using reflected optical microscope (BF-DF)

steps.description: Mount samples on stubs with carbon tapes and sputter-coat with a thin layer of gold (10-15 nm) for SEM analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare vegetal fiber samples for SEM (mounting, coating)

steps.description: Observe both transversal and paradermal surfaces using SEM.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe transversal and paradermal surfaces using SEM

steps.description: Describe microanatomical observations based on OM and SEM analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Describe microanatomical features from microscopy

steps.description: Compare archaeological sample descriptions with modern materials.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare fiber microanatomy with modern materials

steps.description: Compare archaeological sample descriptions with specialized technical literature.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare fiber microanatomy with technical literature

methods.description: Identification of archaeological Underground Storage Organs (USOs) based on detailed description of observable anatomical structures, using outer features for complete specimens and internal anatomy (vascular tissue, parenchyma cells) requiring SEM for fragmented ones.

steps.description: Describe the observable anatomical structures of the USO sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Describe anatomical structures of USO sample

steps.description: For small-sized tubers that are complete, examine the outer features, such as shape or detachment scars.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine outer features of complete USO tubers

steps.description: For fragmented storage organs, examine the internal anatomy, especially the organization of vascular tissue and anatomy of parenchyma cells.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine internal anatomy of fragmented USOs (vascular tissue, parenchyma)

steps.description: Use SEM for examining the internal anatomy of fragmented storage organs.
steps.searchable_summary: Use SEM for internal anatomy examination of fragmented USOs

methods.description: Analysis of pollen and non-pollen palynomorphs (NPP) from archaeological and lacustrine deposits to reconstruct vegetation history and environmental conditions, involving standard sample processing, identification using atlases and literature, and analysis of pollen data for plant use identification.

steps.description: Process samples following standard methods.
steps.searchable_summary: Process palynological samples using standard methods

steps.description: Identify 300-400 pollen grains of terrestrial taxa per sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify terrestrial pollen grains (300-400 per sample)

steps.description: Identify pollen from hygrophytes and aquatic plants.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify hygrophyte and aquatic plant pollen

steps.description: Identify non-pollen palynomorphs (NPP).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify non-pollen palynomorphs (NPP)

steps.description: Identify pollen according to pollen atlas.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify pollen using pollen atlas

steps.description: Identify pollen according to specialized literature.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify pollen using specialized literature

steps.description: Analyze pollen data to reconstruct vegetation history at local (archaeological sites) and regional (lacustrine deposits) scales.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze pollen data for vegetation history reconstruction

steps.description: Analyze pollen data to reconstruct environmental conditions at the local scale (pollen from hygrophytes and aquatic plants and non-pollen palynomorphs-NPP).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze pollen/NPP data for local environmental reconstruction

steps.description: Analyze pollen data to identify plant uses in archaeological sites, given the overrepresentation of herbaceous pollen in this type of context, enabling the identification of crops and gathered species.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze pollen data for plant use identification (crops, gathered species)
